I had one of the worst experiences with CelcomDigi. First, the company sells used phone numbers that were clearly registered by previous owners on other platforms. This makes it nearly impossible to verify your number on apps or online accounts, wasting hours of time. When I called customer service for help, they were dishonest and unhelpful, repeatedly trying to blame me or other platforms even after I provided proof that my number was recycled. Instead of offering real support, they told me to pay extra just to receive a simple e-invoice proving I owned the number — despite having already answered all their security questions twice. It felt like they were deliberately adding friction rather than solving the issue. Their final “solution” was to tell me to visit a physical office, with no guarantee they could fix it — even though all I wanted was a basic ownership confirmation for the number I paid for. The most alarming part? The day after I activated the number, I received a call from a scammer claiming to be the previous owner — they somehow already knew the number had been reactivated. That raises serious concerns about data security and privacy, especially since the SIM was purchased at the airport where they also took my passport details. 💬 Foreigners, beware. If you buy a SIM from CelcomDigi, you might end up with a recycled number, constant verification issues, scam calls, and zero accountability from customer support. It’s simply not worth the frustration.

When I requested to switch providers due to their failure to deliver the promised speed, they refused to waive the contractual penalty. In short, I advise everyone to avoid this company at all costs. They lock you into a contract, charge you monthly, but fail to provide the service they promised. Do not sign up for their home fiber service unless you want frustration and poor internet connectivity.

On the 6th, I requested a 3-line port-out from DG to Brand M. By the 8th, two of the lines received SMS requests and I replied to them. However, one line (under my father’s number) did not receive any SMS to reply. - 1st call to DG helpline: They told me to wait 2 hours. - 2nd call to DG helpline: They told me to wait 6 hours. When I asked why, they said the system showed “pending” but couldn’t give me a clear answer. Instead, they asked me to go to Brand M’s counter. Of course, my main problem is that no one is helping my father with the DG SMS reply system.(The system like my father age) The issue is that my father(Old GEN) uses an old 3310 and doesn’t know how to reply to SMS. DG claims to be a 5G provider, but their system still runs on LTE, which causes problems. Honestly, it feels like they’re just treating customers like a ball, passing us around without solving the issue. Meanwhile, DG kept calling me with cheaper offers—like RM35 per line for unlimited data and calls. (Tip: if you try to port out, they’ll suddenly offer you special deals "i cant promise but your guys can try" haha) On the 13th, I went back to my hometown to help my father reply to the port-out SMS. On the 14th, I received a message saying I needed to make a HOTbill payment within 2 hours, which I completed on time. Then came the next problem: I received another message saying the port-out was rejected due to contract issues (my contract still has 1–2 years left). When I called DG helpline, they said it would take 72 hours to investigate. According to SOP, I now need to reply to the SMS system again. In reality, DG is just holding me back and wasting my time. That’s why—even with cheaper plans—I still want to quit. Above is my story. Until now, no one has helped me solve it, and I don’t understand why it takes so long to handle such a simple matter. If anyone has faced the same case, please teach me how to settle it and advise me on who I can report to

I am an end-user of Telco service in Malaysia. I have decided to change from Maxis to CelcomDigi for my Internet fibre service. On 21 Dec 2025, CelcomDigi put up a request to transfer from Maxis to CelcomDigi. However, the application has been pending for few days awaiting approval of transfer from Maxis before CelcomDigi can do the transfer setup at my home. I have been following up with Maxis Service Hotline on this matter and also log in call with their customer service. The careline operators asked me to wait for their technical team's call. On 27 Dec 2025, the technical call me at 3.30pm and subsequent 2 calls within 5minutes,but I missed their calls. However, I called back without response from them but managed to receive their call on 4.51pm. The customer service officer informed me that the application of transfer has been approved. However, after 6minutes later, he called me again and informed that my application has been rejected due to the previous miss call. He asked me to request CelcomDigi to put up the application again. What is the rediculuos process that Maxis wanted to create trouble to end-user like me.

The most inflexible company on the planet. For context, I am a doctor working in the UK but needing to keep my Malaysian phone number (as tied to my bank accounts). I have been paying 28MYR/month to keep the number active. I have found that hotlink will allow you to pay a flat rate of 30MYR per year to keep the number active on a pre-paid sim. I then proceeded to start porting my number out. This has been one of the most difficult administrative tasks I have ever done. In order to port out, I was made to pay FOUR different bills - each time I was told that that specific payment would clear my balance. On top of that, when asking for these payments, you are only given 2 HOURS to fill pay it before you have to restart the entire process. I am a surgeon, what if I was operating?? Am I expected to wait around all day for this SMS and pay it on the spot? Otherwise my “port-out” request is rejected?? Horrible scam of a company that has terrible service, is terrible value for money and makes it almost impossible to leave. Would HIGHLY recommend to steer clear if possible. TLDR - if you are a Malaysian based in the UK, use a hotlink pantas pre-paid sim to maintain your number. Dont bother with this absolute scam of a company.

As mentioned by others, customer support is non-existent and is a genuine hassle to go through. You quite literally have to figure everything out on your own and will always have to attend to their store in person to modify anything with your telecommunication service. Online support is not available at all and they have no idea about their own system as you could inquire and send multiple tickets and get different answer by different people but none of them will be helpful to you as its going to be them telling what you could do but never how to proceed. Therefore you will be stuck in a loop of questions and none of them will read the history of your inquiry and repeat the same answer whenever you ask for further clarification. Its worse than an AI answer. They take 1-3 days to answer one question everytime. Sometimes even more. They will also abruptly close tickets without warning thus losing all the progress and restarting multiple tickets. You will spend more than a month to resolve anything and close to impossible if you are someone who have to travel overseas alot as you cant attend their store in person to modify your services. This in turn will result you to paying subscriptions for the months spent going back and forth with customer service and wasting money on services you could not unsubscribe from throughout the entire ordeal. Stay clear of this service.

The Digi app is incredibly inconvenient. If you want to check back on your bills and leave the chat, you will have to reenter your Ticket Number and OTP even though you have not exited the app itself. When you are outside of Malaysia you can only rely on email OTPs as the sms OTPs would not arrive in time for you to use it. Even the email OTPs do not always work right off the bat and you'll have to request another code.

I have been a loyal Digi customer for several years on a prepaid plan with the Super Longlife feature. I have never opted for Auto Reload due to my frequent time spent overseas, but I need to keep my number active to pay bills in Malaysia. My Super Longlife feature expired on December 24th, so I reloaded RM50 to renew it for another year. However, Digi used the RM50 to auto-renew a Rombo 35 plan, which I never agreed to. I contacted Digi via messaging, and they deactivated the Rombo 35 plan but have refused to reimburse the RM50. This feels dishonest. I called Digi, and I was told I would receive a refund, but just a short while ago, I received an auto-reply saying they are unable to process the refund. What should I do next?”
